title: Support --dns=none like podman
body:
This PR adds support for `--dns=none`, which will avoid setting up a bind mount for `/etc/resolv.conf`. This is equivalent to podman's `--dns=none`, which was added in https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2747

It looks like it was possible at one point to avoid a bind mount on `/etc/resolv.conf` as a side-effect of passing network configuration options in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1146 but it appears this way has been factored out of the code since then. This gives "I don't want resolv.conf" a dedicated knob to twist, which is shaped and labeled identically to the knob in podman :)

I may eventually want to add an equivalent podman's of `--no-hosts` in a separate PR, but I left that off for now since it would be a bit more involved and since I don't personally need it just yet.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

Can you update the man page of `buildah from` and `buildah bud` to include this documentation?  
Should we throw an error if the user specifies two DNS Options with one of them being NONE?

@jordemort You also need to sign your commit.

git commit -a -s --amend
git push --force
@rhatdan I think you meant update man for buildah from and buildah bud

@rhatdan The commit is signed, with key ID C341950C47B6CE14 - maybe because I wasn't in the whitelist?

I'll add in the documentation changes.

If the user specifies `--dns=none,somethingelse` then the length check won't match and `buildah` will go ahead and try and use `none` as a DNS server.  Eventually this will lead to the following error message when the string `none` is passed to `getDNSIP`:

```error getting DNS servers: invalid IP address none```
Stole the documentation for this out of the podman docs. Also added the `--dns*` options to `buildah-from.md`, which was missing them entirely.

@jordemort The validator is looking for a string like:

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

In your commit.

Also can you squash you PR's together.

I think I can do

buildah from --dns none --dns 8.8.8.8 fedora 

Which should throw an error.

@rhatdan Ok, I will squash and add the DCO

Multiple `--dns` options seem to be the same as a single `--dns` option with multiple values:

```
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah from --dns none --dns 8.8.8.8 docker.io/fedora
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 34b3503aa97f done
Copying config 9754ce1464 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
fedora-working-container
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah run -- fedora-working-container bash
error getting dns servers: invalid IP address none
```

Note that we don't hit the error until `buildah run`; I wonder if it would be better to add something to catch this and error at `buildah from`?
@jordemort That is what I want, catch it in buildah bud and buildah run CLI processing rather then waiting for a container to run.  I don't think you can fix it after the fact.

Added some stuff in `pkg/parse/parse.go` to catch conflicts. Go isn't even my 10th best language so please let me know if my code is ugly.

```
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah from --dns none --dns 8.8.8.8 docker.io/fedora
invalid --dns, --dns=none may not be used with any other --dns options
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah from --dns none --dns-search chicken docker.io/fedora
invalid --dns-search, --dns-search may not be used with --dns=none
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah from --dns none --dns-option chicken docker.io/fedora
invalid --dns-option, --dns-option may not be used with --dns=none
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah from --dns none docker.io/fedora
fedora-working-container-1
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah run -- fedora-working-container-1 cat /etc/resolv.conf 
# Generated by NetworkManager
nameserver 192.168.122.1
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# cat /etc/resolv.conf
# This file is included on the metadata iso
nameserver 192.168.65.1
domain webb.haus
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah from --dns 8.8.8.8 docker.io/fedora
fedora-working-container-2
root@a0343e7ab207:/app# buildah run -- fedora-working-container-2 cat /etc/resolv.conf 
nameserver 8.8.8.8
```
I had `if len(dnsServers) > 1` instead of `if noDNS && len(dnsServers) > 1`, which would have flat-out prevented multiple DNS servers at all. Fixed.
